infobox.value: (Dutch pronunciation: [[ˈdrɔŋə(n)]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Nl-Drongen.ogg "File:Nl-Drongen.ogg"); [French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Tronchiennes*, pronounced [[tʁɔ̃ʃjɛn]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French")) is a [sub-municipality](/wiki/Sub-municipality "Sub-municipality") of the [city](/wiki/City_status_in_Belgium "City status in Belgium") of [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent") located in the [province of East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"), [Flemish Region](/wiki/Flemish_Region "Flemish Region"), [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium"). It was a separate [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Belgium "Municipalities of Belgium") until 1977. In 1967, part of the original municipality was already annexed to Ghent.[[1]](#cite_note-1) On 1 January 1977, the municipality of Drongen was [merged](/wiki/Fusion_of_the_Belgian_municipalities "Fusion of the Belgian municipalities") into Ghent.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
["LISTE ALPHABETIQUE DES COMMUNES - Fusions de 1963 à 1977"](https://doc.statbel.fgov.be/publications/M000.03/M000.03FN_Comm_Gem_1963-1977.pdf) (PDF).

Sub-municipality of the city of Ghent, Belgium

**Drongen** (Dutch pronunciation: [[ˈdrɔŋə(n)]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Nl-Drongen.ogg "File:Nl-Drongen.ogg"); [French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Tronchiennes*, pronounced [[tʁɔ̃ʃjɛn]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French")) is a [sub-municipality](/wiki/Sub-municipality "Sub-municipality") of the [city](/wiki/City_status_in_Belgium "City status in Belgium") of [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent") located in the [province of East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"), [Flemish Region](/wiki/Flemish_Region "Flemish Region"), [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium"). It was a separate [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Belgium "Municipalities of Belgium") until 1977. In 1967, part of the original municipality was already annexed to Ghent.[[1]](#cite_note-1) On 1 January 1977, the municipality of Drongen was [merged](/wiki/Fusion_of_the_Belgian_municipalities "Fusion of the Belgian municipalities") into Ghent.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Drongen is divided into three parishes: Drongen, [Luchteren](/wiki/Luchteren "Luchteren") and [Baarle](/w/index.php?title=Baarle,_East_Flanders&action=edit&redlink=1 "Baarle, East Flanders (page does not exist)").

## Monastery

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Drongen&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Monastery")]

Drongen is known for its early medieval monastery, [Drongen Abbey](/wiki/Drongen_Abbey "Drongen Abbey"), founded in the 7th century by the monk [Amandus](/wiki/Amandus "Amandus"), the Missionary of the Leie and Schelde. Destroyed by the [Normans](/wiki/Normans "Normans") in 853, the monastery was rebuilt by the [counts of Flanders](/wiki/Counts_of_Flanders "Counts of Flanders"). The monastery was the victim of the religious wars following the [Reformation](/wiki/Protestant_Reformation "Protestant Reformation"), and in 1578 it was once again destroyed by [Calvinists](/wiki/Calvinism "Calvinism").

In 1638, the abbey church was rebuilt and between 1638 and 1698 the monastery was restored. After a fire in 1727, the church tower was restored once again in 1734, with a distinctive appearance. In 1797, the [French](/wiki/French_people "French people") occupied and sold the abbey. In 1804, [Lieven Bauwens](/wiki/Lieven_Bauwens "Lieven Bauwens") used the monastery as a textile plant. The current monastery and abbey church date from 1859 and remain in use as a spiritual centre run by the [Society of Jesus](/wiki/Society_of_Jesus "Society of Jesus") dedicated to the teachings of [Ignatius of Loyola](/wiki/Ignatius_of_Loyola "Ignatius of Loyola").

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Drongen&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Notable people")]

Drongen is the birthplace of professional [footballer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") [Kevin De Bruyne](/wiki/Kevin_De_Bruyne "Kevin De Bruyne").
